quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Danube River flows through Budapest, Hungary's capital, dividing it into two historic parts: Buda on the west bank and Pest on the east. The beautiful Chain Bridge, completed in 1849, was the first permanent bridge to connect these two sides and remains one of the city's most iconic landmarks.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals are common visitors to the Strait of Juan de Fuca, but few people know about their remarkable diving abilities and caring nature. These charming marine mammals have surprising adaptations that help them thrive in Pacific Northwest waters.

medium_read.title: Growing a Garden for Hummingbirds
medium_read.teaser: Learn which flowers and plants attract these tiny jeweled visitors to your garden throughout the seasons. With the right choices, you can create a hummingbird haven that brings color and life to your outdoor space all summer long.

long_read.title: The Restoration of the Elwha River
long_read.teaser: The removal of two dams on the Elwha River near Port Angeles was the largest dam removal project in U.S. history, restoring the river to its natural flow. This remarkable story of environmental restoration shows how nature can heal when given the chance, bringing salmon back to waters they hadn't reached in over a century.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer in Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: May is a wonderful time to spot black-tailed deer and their adorable fawns in Hurricane Ridge's meadows. These gentle creatures are most active in the early morning and evening hours, often seen grazing on wildflowers as the mountain landscape comes alive with spring colors. The drive up offers breathtaking views of the Strait of Juan de Fuca on clear days.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart works incredibly hard to power those amazing wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute — an astonishing pace for such a tiny bird!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The answer is 1,200 beats per minute! It's hard to imagine anything that fast, but that's what it takes to hover in mid-air!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To power their incredibly fast wing beats (up to 80 times per second), hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of any animal. They must consume about half their body weight in sugar daily, visiting hundreds of flowers each day.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! You got it right! It's such a warm and expressive word, isn't it?
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'Köszönöm'! It's one of the most important words to know in Hungarian — expressing gratitude with warmth.
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, part of the Finno-Ugric family rather than the Indo-European languages that surround it. This makes it quite different from neighboring languages like German or Romanian.

puzzle_3.question: What year did Olympic National Park officially become a national park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes, 1938! President Franklin D. Roosevelt established Olympic National Park to protect its incredible rainforests, mountains, and coastline for generations to come.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It was 1938! President Roosevelt created the park to preserve the Olympic Peninsula's unique ecosystems — from temperate rainforests to alpine peaks to wild coastline.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Olympic National Park protects nearly one million acres of wilderness, including some of the finest remaining temperate rainforest in the United States. The park is also home to the largest unmanaged herd of Roosevelt elk in the Pacific Northwest.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
